summary: Momentum is mass in motion, p = m·v. It is the natural quantity for collisions and pushes, because the total momentum of an isolated system is conserved.
method: Identify the moving object and note its mass and velocity.
Decide which quantity is unknown — momentum, mass, or velocity.
Rearrange p = m·v for that unknown (v = p/m or m = p/v).
Use SI units (kg, m/s) so the momentum comes out in kg·m/s.
Keep track of direction: momentum is a vector, so a sign tells you which way it points.

example.title: Momentum of a rolling ball
steps: The unknown is p, so use p = m·v.
